Discipline Differential Analysis
Spring Arbor University | Faculty Compensation Cost Analysis | CUPA 2025-26 Data

This report compares salary levels by discipline to inform decisions about discipline differentials in the compensation structure. All data are drawn from the CUPA-HR Faculty Salary Survey for tenured and tenure-track faculty only; non-tenure-track, adjunct, and clinical faculty are excluded. The salary factor is a ratio of a discipline's institutional average salary to the all-disciplines institutional average within a given comparison group. A factor of 1.20, for example, means the discipline's average salary is 20% above the all-disciplines average. The CUPA reports used here are configured to compare institutional average salaries.

Comparison groups presented:

All Institutions 2025-26:
4-digit CIP: 629 institutions; 6-digit CIP: 452 institutions; all faculty degrees
All Institutions 2025-26 (Doctoral):
4-digit CIP: 598 institutions; filtered to Doctoral degree holders
All Institutions 2025-26 (Masters):
4-digit CIP: 562 institutions; filtered to Masters degree holders
Masters Institutions 2025-26:
4-digit CIP: 86 institutions; 6-digit CIP: 86 institutions; all faculty degrees
Masters Institutions 2025-26 (Doctoral):
4-digit CIP: 86 institutions; filtered to Doctoral degree holders
Masters Institutions 2025-26 (Masters):
4-digit CIP: 86 institutions; filtered to Masters degree holders

Disciplines are grouped into four clusters based on their average Masters Institutions 2025-26 salary factor across ranks:

High Premium (1.15+):
Strong candidates for discipline differentials.
Moderate Premium (1.05–1.14):
May warrant differentials depending on institutional priorities and market competition.
Near Average (0.95–1.04):
Generally no differential needed.
Below Average (< 0.95):
Discipline pays below the all-disciplines average. No upward differential warranted.
Data robustness: Entries shown in italic gray are based on fewer than 10 reporting institutions. Per CUPA policy, statistics based on fewer than 5 institutions are suppressed entirely and appear as dashes. When 5–9 institutions report, results are shown but should be interpreted with caution.
Mean–median divergence (†): Because the salary factor is based on institutional averages, it can be pulled upward by a small number of high-paying institutions. Disciplines marked with † have a comparison group average that exceeds the median by 5% or more at one or more ranks. This typically occurs in disciplines where a mix of Masters-level and Doctoral-level faculty are compensated at meaningfully different rates. In these disciplines, the salary factor may overstate the salary level experienced by the typical institution. The median salary shown for comparison groups provides a more robust central estimate.
Exploratory disciplines (◆): Disciplines marked with ◆ are not currently offered at SAU. They are included for benchmarking purposes only and show comparison group data (no institutional data is available). These rows appear with a light blue background in the tables below.
Discipline Cluster Summary
The table below groups disciplines by salary factor cluster using the Assistant Professor (incl New) salary factor from the Masters Institutions 2025-26 comparison group. Simple and weighted averages are shown for each cluster; the weighted average uses SAU’s own faculty headcount (NP) per discipline.

Salary Factor at the Assistant Professor (incl New) rank from the Masters Institutions 2025-26 comparison group.
SAU NP = total faculty headcount across all ranks reported to CUPA in this discipline.
Simple Average = unweighted mean of salary factors and median salaries within the cluster.
Weighted Average = mean weighted by SAU’s faculty headcount (NP) in each discipline. Applies to both salary factors and median salaries. Disciplines with more faculty have proportionally greater influence on the cluster average.
* = Salary factor not available in the Masters Institutions 2025-26 comparison group at this rank; factor shown is from the comparison group with the most reporting institutions.
◆ = Exploratory discipline (not currently at SAU). NP is assumed to be 2 for weighting purposes.
4-Digit CIP Analysis
The 4-digit CIP level groups related sub-disciplines together (e.g., all of Computer Science rather than specific sub-fields). This provides broader data coverage and is typically the appropriate level for setting discipline differentials.

Salary Factor = ratio of this discipline’s institutional average salary to the all-disciplines institutional average within each comparison group. Factor > 1.00 = discipline pays above average; < 1.00 = below average.
NI = number of institutions reporting; NP = number of persons (incumbents). Data in italic indicates fewer than 10 institutions reported (interpret with caution).
Cluster assignment is based on the average Masters Institutions 2025-26 salary factor across available ranks (or fallback group if data is unavailable).
† = Mean–median divergence ≥ 5% at one or more ranks. The salary factor (based on averages) may overstate typical pay in these disciplines; see median salary for a more robust estimate.
◆ = Exploratory discipline (not currently offered at SAU). Comparison group data only; no institutional data.
Discipline Cluster Summary — 6-digit CIP
The table below groups 6-digit disciplines by salary factor cluster using the Assistant Professor (incl New) salary factor from the Masters Institutions 2025-26 comparison group. Fewer disciplines may appear here than in the 4-digit summary because 6-digit coverage depends on whether the institution reported at that level of detail and whether comparison group sample sizes were large enough for CUPA to publish results.

Salary Factor at the Assistant Professor (incl New) rank from the Masters Institutions 2025-26 comparison group.
SAU NP = total faculty headcount across all ranks reported to CUPA in this discipline.
Simple Average = unweighted mean of salary factors and median salaries within the cluster.
Weighted Average = mean weighted by SAU’s faculty headcount (NP) in each discipline. Applies to both salary factors and median salaries. Disciplines with more faculty have proportionally greater influence on the cluster average.
* = Salary factor not available in the Masters Institutions 2025-26 comparison group at this rank; factor shown is from the comparison group with the most reporting institutions.
◆ = Exploratory discipline (not currently at SAU). NP is assumed to be 2 for weighting purposes.
6-Digit CIP Analysis
The 6-digit CIP level shows specific sub-disciplines. Some of SAU's disciplines map to different 6-digit codes than the 4-digit grouping, which can reveal sub-discipline variation. Note that the 6-digit comparison group sizes differ from the 4-digit groups.

Salary Factor = ratio of this discipline’s institutional average salary to the all-disciplines institutional average within each comparison group. Factor > 1.00 = discipline pays above average; < 1.00 = below average.
NI = number of institutions reporting; NP = number of persons (incumbents). Data in italic indicates fewer than 10 institutions reported (interpret with caution).
Cluster assignment is based on the average Masters Institutions 2025-26 salary factor across available ranks (or fallback group if data is unavailable).
† = Mean–median divergence ≥ 5% at one or more ranks. The salary factor (based on averages) may overstate typical pay in these disciplines; see median salary for a more robust estimate.
◆ = Exploratory discipline (not currently offered at SAU). Comparison group data only; no institutional data.

Generated from CUPA-HR Faculty Salary Survey 2025-26 data (tenured/tenure-track faculty). Report prepared April 2026 for Spring Arbor University.
